section: ENG9_4 – In the Kingdom of Fools
title: In the Kingdom of Fools – Flashcard Concept Mastery
author: A.K. Ramanujan

questions.question: Who were the rulers of the Kingdom of Fools?
questions.correct_answer: A foolish king and his minister
questions.explanation: Both the king and the minister were described as idiots who ruled the kingdom with irrational decisions.

questions.question: What strange rule did the king introduce in the kingdom?
questions.correct_answer: People had to work at night and sleep during the day
questions.explanation: The king ordered everyone to reverse their daily routine, turning night into day and day into night.

questions.question: What surprised the guru and his disciple in the market?
questions.correct_answer: Everything cost the same price, one duddu
questions.explanation: All goods, whether cheap or expensive, were sold at the same price.

questions.question: Why did the guru decide to leave the kingdom?
questions.correct_answer: Because it was a kingdom of fools and unsafe
questions.explanation: The guru understood that foolish rulers could create unpredictable dangers.

questions.question: Why did the disciple stay in the kingdom?
questions.explanation: The disciple was tempted by the cheap food and ignored the guru’s advice.

questions.question: What caused the thief’s death in the story?
questions.correct_answer: A weak wall collapsed on him
questions.explanation: The thief was trying to steal from a merchant’s house when the wall fell on him.

questions.question: Who were blamed in the court case for the thief’s death?
questions.correct_answer: The merchant, bricklayer, dancer and goldsmith
questions.explanation: The blame kept shifting from one person to another due to the king’s foolish judgement.

questions.question: Why was the disciple chosen for execution?
questions.correct_answer: Because he was fat enough to fit the execution stake
questions.explanation: The king needed someone who physically fit the stake meant for execution.

questions.question: How did the guru save his disciple?
questions.correct_answer: He tricked the king by saying the first person to die would be reborn as king
questions.explanation: The guru used clever psychology to fool the king and minister.

questions.question: What happened to the king and the minister in the end?
questions.correct_answer: They were executed on the stake themselves
questions.explanation: Believing the guru’s trick, they secretly took the place of the prisoners and were executed.

notes.title: Guru’s Wisdom
notes.content: The guru represents intelligence and foresight, recognising danger in foolish leadership.

notes.title: Satire on Leadership
notes.content: The story humorously criticises rulers who make irrational decisions without thinking of consequences.

facts.fact: The story is a Kannada folktale retold by A.K. Ramanujan.
The kingdom’s rule made people live opposite to the natural daily cycle.
